summary: The book's biggest live risk is operational, not a fresh filing: the nightly audit shows two HELD names' price caches badly desynced from the broker of record (WRAP cache 1.555 last 7/8 vs broker 2.18 = 38.9%; NTSK cache 12.61 vs broker 14.49 = 14.2%), and WRAP's stop line $2.03 sits ABOVE the stale cache -- a stop-check reading that cache could phantom-sell a +$3.11 winner at a fake loss; underneath, the standing distribution overhang persists (UUUU $300M ATM undrawn, WRAP $200M baby-shelf, NTSK post-lockup Form 144/VC sales) but a same-cycle news re-sweep confirms NO fresh July offering on any held name and broker_state reconciles all 5 positions on shares.

WRAP.note: DATA-QUALITY driven: nightly audit flags HELD broker_disagrees 38.9% (cache 1.555 last 7/8 vs broker 2.16-2.18 / alpaca 2.19) + stale_cache; stop line $2.03 sits ABOVE the stale 1.555, so a stop-check on that cache could phantom-sell this +$3.11 winner -- dashboard/exit-radar themselves use the correct 2.19. Dilution: $200M universal baby-shelf standing on a sub-$100M post-pump name = drip CAPACITY only; re-sweep confirms NO July 424B5/ATM draw (only WrapShield launch + ATF-ruling PR). Recent insider activity is BUYING (CEO). FinBERT +0.019. Position +$3.11 at $2.18.

UUUU.note: $300M Controlled Equity ATM (June-2025 program #333-278193) confirmed still undrawn -- no July prospectus draw. Insider picture net-NEGATIVE by size: Chalmers Form 144 stack via RBC (~800k sh 6/29-7/9, ~$10M+) outweighs CEO Bhappu's 74k/$968k buy (7/7) + Hansen (7/8). Lorentzian k-NN -0.75, FinBERT 0.0. Position -$1.18 at $13.30.

CAI.note: Fundamentals clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no shelf/ATM). Not in tonight's audit -- broker $17.36 vs cache converged. FinBERT +0.144 (best +0.861 PTEN/FDA item). Reconciles on shares. Position +$0.65 at $17.36.

NTSK.note: Book's best gainer +$7.09 at $14.49 on an intact breakout, but RAISED on DATA QUALITY: audit flags HELD broker_disagrees 14.2% (cache 12.61 vs broker 14.49) -- stops/P&L off the cache understate the position; live feed shows correct 14.49. Post-IPO-lockup distribution persists (Form 144 1.75M Class A 7/8 + Lightspeed VC sales 7/8-7/9); ICONIQ/Makan/Griffith buys + 241k director buy only partly offset. Re-sweep: NO 424B5/secondary filing. Hiring pulse 148 (prev 150), flat -- no posting-pull.

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing; failed Feb-2026 take-private replaced by anti-dilutive $140M self-tender. Audit shows only a minor 3.9% broker_disagrees (cache 5.22 vs broker 5.43) -- immaterial to the $5.00 stop. Hiring pulse 25 (prev 24), flat. Position +$0.75 at $5.49.

flags.why: Distressed reverse-merger pop: definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Hiring pulse now 0 open roles (was null) -- corroborates the wind-down. On the scan (4.56) and the daily-research avoid list.

Most recent 10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant overhang). FinBERT -0.288 on the cash-runway story. Keep hard-vetoed if it resurfaces on the scan.

Top-3 scan candidate (6.17, just below the 6.25 line) that diluted ~30%: $50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement closed 6/26, plus a 7/6 F-3 registering 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. Stock ~$1.70 sits BELOW the $1.81 offer; the CEO's 7/7 buy came AFTER the raise. No NEW July offering. Intel concurs (flags_for_bot).

Top-3 scan candidate (6.1, below line) with a textbook pump-and-dump signature: HK microcap ran ~$0.23 (6/12) to >$10 (6/16) then collapsed to ~$3.59, no fundamental driver. Reject on the pump veto; nightly audit also flags cache stale (last 7/8). Intel concurs (flags_for_bot).

Top-3 non-held scan name (5.17, below the 6.25 line) with real recent dilution: $300M underwritten offering -- 23,871,526 sh at $11.52 + 2,170,138 pre-funded warrants -- closed ~5/11/2026. Stock ~$9.35 sits BELOW the $11.52 offer; the +4.0 insider score is almost certainly offering-backer/healthcare-fund accumulation, NOT management conviction. Do not buy as a fresh insider breakout.

Scan candidate (4.46) on the avoid list with a severe dilution overhang: ~167M sh out, active $300M S-3 shelf (7/8), 13.95M warrants, authorized shares hiked to 450M. Phase 2 news constructive but capital structure unresolved.
Scan candidate (4.08) on the avoid list: automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 2026-01-13, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup.

Material dilution + binary-vote risk; intel concurs do-not-chase.

On the avoid list, not held: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, 10-for-1 reverse split tied to Nasdaq compliance, pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- distress signal, not a turnaround.

No longer held and off the scan; last cycle's ledger-only broker desync is RESOLVED (broker reports 5 positions, all reconcile, DVLT closed out). Retained as a do-not-REBUY: serial diluter with active pump activity (Mandela Dollar stablecoin 7/10, Dream Bowl meme-coin 7/6). FinBERT +0.455 on GPU-fleet pump headlines.

disagreements: DATA QUALITY (not in intel): the nightly audit flags two HELD names' price caches badly stale vs the broker of record -- WRAP cache 1.555 (last 7/8) vs broker 2.16-2.18 = 38.9%, NTSK cache 12.61 vs broker 14.49 = 14.2%. WRAP's stop line $2.03 is ABOVE the stale cache, so a stop evaluation on that cache could phantom-sell a profitable position; intel's buy-side view misses this. Reconciler/human must confirm the live-price feed feeds the stop logic BEFORE the next stop check.

UUUU: intel frames the Bhappu/Hansen insider BUYS prominently and carries 'no boost'; the Chalmers Form 144 SALE stack (~800k sh 6/29-7/9 via RBC, ~$10M+) outweighs the ~$1M of buying by size, so the insider signal behind the score is net-NEGATIVE -- reinforces the elevated grade.

NTSK: intel narrates the ICONIQ/Makan/Griffith BUYS as the story; the offsetting Lightspeed VC sales (7/8-7/9) plus the 7/8 Form 144 (1.75M Class A) are a live post-lockup distribution overhang -- the net float pressure is why this stays elevated despite the +$7.09 gain.

ARTV: intel does not cover it; the +4.0 insider score driving its 5.17 scan rank is offering-backer/healthcare-fund accumulation into a $300M raise the stock is now underwater on (~$9.35 vs $11.52 offer), NOT management conviction -- treat as dilution overhang, not a buy signal.

AVO: intel carries it as a top non-held name on a +5.0 insider score, but that signal is GlobalHarvest/Jay Pack takeover-style accumulation that triggered a poison-pill rights plan, NOT management conviction; below the buy line so no flag, but the insider component is mis-attributed.
Overtrading (carried, unchanged): radar shows weekly buy budget 9/4 used -- more than double the 4-buy weekly cap. Standalone discipline flag independent of any single ticker.
